wiki.summary: “HELLRAISER” showcases Che’s new vocal capabilities, seeing him reach new octaves with his voice reminiscent to OsamaSon, who is featured on the song, and takes up the middle portion of the track. The track is produced by Che’s frequent collaborator CXO, and production features glitchy synths with chaotic percussion and heavy-hitting bass, which blend perfectly with Che and OsamaSon’s vocals and adlibs. On May 14, Che would preview the track on his X page. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “HELLRAISER” showcases Che’s new vocal capabilities, seeing him reach new octaves with his voice reminiscent to OsamaSon, who is featured on the song, and takes up the middle portion of the track. The track is produced by Che’s frequent collaborator CXO, and production features glitchy synths with chaotic percussion and heavy-hitting bass, which blend perfectly with Che and OsamaSon’s vocals and adlibs. On May 14, Che would preview the track on his X page. He would go on to play snippets of the song at Summer Smash and at Clout Festival Poland, making fans more excited about the song. On July 14, Che and OsamaSon posted a picture of themself, leaving fans speculating that OsamaSon would have a feature on the song, which would be confirmed shortly after. Two days before the REST IN BASS release, on the night of July 15, 2025, Che and OsamaSon posted Instagram stories with a YouTube link, which would reveal the music video if you clicked on it. The music video features anolog-horror-like graphics such as shown in the REST IN BASS trailers, and was directed by Che’s and OsamaSon’s main directors, PoppyGavin, noahsocold, Gekaay and stan.u.u. The track would officially release for all streaming platforms on Che’s sophomore studio album, REST IN BASS, on July 18, 2025. On August 31, 2025, NamelessMeem, an user from the Che subreddit, discovered that the sample being used in “HELLRAISER” was the song “spur” by the band Greenhouse. Read more on Last.fm.
